heading: Supervision of military housing by Chief Housing Officer
label: 2851a Supervision of military housing by Chief Housing Officer
notes: Amendments2022—Subsec. (a). amended subsec. (a) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows:Pub. L. 117–263“(1) The Secretary of Defense shall designate, from among officials of the Department of Defense who are appointed by the President with the advice and consent of the Senate, a Chief Housing Officer who shall oversee family housing and military unaccompanied housing under the jurisdiction of the Department of Defense or acquired or constructed under subchapter IV of this chapter (in this section referred to as ‘covered housing units’).“(2) The official of the Department of Defense designated as Chief Housing Officer may be assigned duties in addition to the duties as Chief Housing Officer under subsection (b).”2021—, renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 116–283, § 2811(a)(3)section 2890a of this title, inserted “Supervision of military housing by” before “Chief” in section catchline.Pub. L. 116–283, § 2811(a)(2)Subsec. (a)(1). , substituted “family housing and military unaccompanied housing under the jurisdiction of the Department of Defense or acquired or constructed under subchapter IV of this chapter (in this section referred to as ‘covered housing units’)” for “housing units”.Pub. L. 116–283, § 2811(a)(1)(A)Subsec. (b)(1). , inserted “covered” before “housing units” in subpars. (A) and (B).Pub. L. 116–283, § 2811(a)(1)(B)(ii), substituted “covered housing units” for “housing under subchapter IV and this subchapter” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 116–283, § 2811(a)(1)(B)(i)

Notification of Designation, , , provided that not later than 60 days after , the Secretary of Defense was to notify the Committees on Armed Services and Appropriations of the Senate and the House of Representatives of the official of the Department of Defense designated as Chief Housing Officer under this section. As amended by , subsec. (a) of this section provides that the Assistant Secretary of Defense for Energy, Installations, and Environment shall serve as the Chief Housing Officer.Pub. L. 116–92, div. B, title XXX, § 3012(b)133 Stat. 1921Pub. L. 117–263Dec. 20, 2019Dec. 20, 2019

children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: The Assistant Secretary of Defense for Energy, Installations, and Environment shall serve as the Chief Housing Officer, who shall oversee family housing and military unaccompanied housing under the jurisdiction of the Department of Defense or acquired or constructed under subchapter IV of this chapter (in this section referred to as “covered housing units”).

children.label: A
children.text: Creation and standardization of policies and processes regarding covered housing units.

children.label: B
children.text: Oversight of the administration of any Department of Defense-wide policies regarding covered housing units, to include, in coordination with the Secretaries of the military departments, the housing documents developed pursuant to entitled Military Housing Privatization Initiative Tenant Bill of Rights and Military Housing Privatization Initiative Tenant Responsibilities.section 2890 of this title
The duties specified in paragraph (1) may not be further delegated.
